title: dryopteris pallida subsp. libanotica
intro: Dryopteris pallida subsp. libanotica, also known as the Lebanese wood fern, is a subspecies of the Dryopteris pallida fern, a type of deciduous to semi-evergreen fern native to the Middle East. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating a range of health issues. The health benefits of Dryopteris pallida subsp. libanotica include reducing inflammation and pain, improving respiratory function, and aiding in the treatment of digestive issues and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically harvested, dried, and used as an infusion or tincture to harness its medicinal properties.
